title: Long numeric literals should use digit separators
messageFormat: Group the digits of this numeric literal with '_' separators to make it readable.
summary: A run of five or more digits with no grouping forces the reader to count places to judge the magnitude of a constant. The underscore digit separator lets the value be scanned the way numbers are normally written.
The separator is ignored by the compiler, so it improves legibility without affecting the literal's value.
